name: Micronesia

visa_free.notes: US citizens may enter FSM to live, work, or study indefinitely without visas or non-citizen registration requirements per the Compact of Free Association (Section 142(a)). No statutory limit on length of stay; entry permit may be issued for up to one year at a time, renewable. US passport must be valid for at least 180 days from date of entry per travel.state.gov guidance.

Under the Agreement between the Federated States of Micronesia and the European Union on the short-stay visa waiver, signed and entered into provisional application 20 September 2016, citizens of EU member states - excluding Ireland, which opted out per protocols annexed to the EU treaties - may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. UK citizens are also excluded following Brexit. Applies to holders of valid ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, or special passports issued by EU member states. Excludes travel for the purpose of carrying out a paid activity. Formalised by Council Decision (EU) 2016/1879 and Council Decision (EU) 2017/234. The UK opted out pre-Brexit and remains excluded post-Brexit. Confirmed by EEAS press announcement and EUR-Lex CELEX:22016A1025(01). Agreement remains in effect as of 2026.

Citizens and nationals of Palau may enter visa-free for up to 30 days without an entry permit. An entry permit may then be issued for the duration of the visit not exceeding one year (same as US citizens; compared with 90 days maximum for general nationalities). Confirmed by FSM Embassy entry permit application page: 'Citizens or nationals of the United States, Republic of Palau and the Republic of the Marshall Islands may be issued an entry permit for the duration of the visit which shall not exceed one year.'

Citizens and nationals of the Marshall Islands may enter visa-free for up to 30 days without an entry permit. An entry permit may then be issued for the duration of the visit not exceeding one year (same as US citizens; compared with 90 days maximum for general nationalities). Confirmed by FSM Embassy entry permit application page: 'Citizens or nationals of the United States, Republic of Palau and the Republic of the Marshall Islands may be issued an entry permit for the duration of the visit which shall not exceed one year.'

visa_policy.notes: FSM operates a de-facto visa-free policy for all nationalities for stays up to 30 days (extendable by entry permit up to 90 days total for general nationalities; up to 1 year for US/Palau/Marshall Islands citizens). US citizens have unlimited stay rights under the Compact of Free Association (no fixed maximum, permit renewable up to one year at a time). EU member state citizens (excluding Ireland and UK) have an enhanced 90-day entitlement per 180-day period under the 2016 bilateral visa waiver agreement - all passport types covered. No e-visa or ETA system exists. No visa-on-arrival fee - entry is free of visa charges for all nationalities. A USD 20 departure tax applies on exit from each island (cash only; diplomatic passport holders exempt per travel.state.gov). UK FCDO confirms passport must be valid at least 120 days after arrival for UK nationals; travel.state.gov specifies 180 days for US citizens.

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of Israel holding valid diplomatic or service passports are exempted from the obligation to obtain a visa for the purpose of entering FSM for a visit or stay not exceeding 90 days within six (6) months from the date of first entry. Nationals intending to stay beyond 90 days must obtain visas in advance. Nationals exercising official functions as diplomatic/consular staff and their household family members are not required to obtain a visa within 30 days of entry; visas for the full period of assignment are required thereafter. Signed in Jerusalem on 21 January 2010 (6th of Shvat 5770).

visa_types.name: Visa-Free Entry (30-Day)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism and leisure visits by all nationalities. No visa or entry permit required for stays of 30 days or less.
visa_types.notes: Valid passport, sufficient funds, and proof of onward travel required. No visa or permit needed for stays of 30 days or less. US citizens need only a valid passport. All nationalities are eligible.

visa_types.name: Entry Permit – General Nationalities
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals (excluding US, Palau, and Marshall Islands citizens) to extend their stay in FSM beyond the initial 30-day visa-free period, up to a maximum of 90 days total.
visa_types.notes: Entry permit required for stays exceeding 30 days. Maximum permitted stay via entry permit is 90 days for general nationalities. Application form (FSM_Entry_Permit_Application.pdf) available from the FSM Embassy. No e-visa or online portal exists. Contact: Division of Immigration & Labor, FSM Department of Justice (passport@doj.gov.fm / +619-320-5844).

visa_types.name: Entry Permit – US, Palau, Marshall Islands Citizens
visa_types.purpose: Allows US citizens and nationals of Palau and the Marshall Islands to stay in FSM for extended periods, with entry permits valid for the duration of the visit up to one year at a time.
visa_types.notes: US citizens have an unlimited right to live, work, and study in FSM under the Compact of Free Association (Section 142(a)); entry permits are renewable with no statutory cap on total stay. Palau and Marshall Islands nationals are entitled to entry permits for up to one year (vs. 90-day cap for other nationalities). Confirmed by FSM Embassy entry permit application page. US passport must be valid for at least 180 days from date of entry per travel.state.gov guidance.

visa_types.name: EU Short-Stay Visa Waiver (90-Day)
visa_types.purpose: Allows citizens of EU member states (excluding Ireland) to visit FSM for up to 90 days in any 180-day period without a visa, under the bilateral EU-FSM visa waiver agreement.
visa_types.notes: Covers holders of ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, and special passports issued by EU member states. Ireland and the UK are excluded and fall under the general 30-day visa-free regime. Does not apply to travel for paid activity. Agreement signed and entered provisional application 20 September 2016. Confirmed in effect as of 2026 per EEAS. Re-confirmed against EUR-Lex CELEX:22016A1025(01) on 2026-06-29.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Service Passport Visa Exemption – Israel
visa_types.purpose: Allows Israeli nationals holding diplomatic or service passports to enter FSM without a visa for visits not exceeding 90 days within any six-month period.
visa_types.notes: Applies only to diplomatic and service passport holders. Ordinary passport holders from Israel fall under the general 30-day visa-free regime. Diplomatic/consular staff and household family members do not need a visa within 30 days of entry; full-term visas required thereafter. Agreement signed 21 January 2010.

visa_types.name: Vessel Entry Permit
visa_types.purpose: Required for vessels (ships) entering FSM waters. Covers crew and passengers aboard private or commercial vessels.
visa_types.notes: Application form available from the FSM Embassy. Required for vessels entering FSM waters.

visa_types.name: Aircraft Entry Permit
visa_types.purpose: Required for aircraft entering FSM airspace/airports, covering crew and passengers on private or chartered aircraft.
visa_types.notes: Application form available from the FSM Embassy. Required for aircraft entering FSM.

cbi.notes: No citizenship by investment programme exists. FSM Constitution prohibits dual citizenship. Naturalisation is possible after 5 years of lawful residence under Article III of the FSM Constitution but this is not an investment programme. No official CBI programme was found on any FSM government source.

agreements: Compact of Free Association with the United States (freedom of movement for US citizens into FSM indefinitely; FSM citizens into USA)

Agreement between the Federated States of Micronesia and the European Union on the Short-Stay Visa Waiver, signed and entered provisional application 20 September 2016 (all passport types - ordinary, diplomatic, service/official, special; 90 days per 180-day period; excludes Ireland and UK; excludes paid activity)

Agreement between the Government of the Federated States of Micronesia and the Government of the State of Israel on Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports, signed 21 January 2010

validation_notes: CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: General 30-day visa-free entry for all nationalities confirmed by live fetch of fsmembassy.fm/for-guests-visitors/ ('Valid passport, sufficient funds and proof of onward travel are sufficient to grant entry for 30 days'). Policy unchanged from prior validation.

CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: US citizen unlimited stay right under Compact of Free Association confirmed by live fetch of travel.state.gov ('U.S. citizens may enter the FSM to live, work, or study indefinitely... There is no limit to the length of time U.S. citizens can remain in the FSM.'). Passport validity 180 days confirmed.

CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: Palau (PLW) and Marshall Islands (MHL) one-year entry permit eligibility confirmed by live fetch of fsmembassy.fm/fsm-entry-permit-application/ ('Citizens or nationals of the United States, Republic of Palau and the Republic of the Marshall Islands may be issued an entry permit for the duration of the visit which shall not exceed one year'). The max_stay_days:30 in visa_free[] correctly represents the initial permit-free entry; the 365-day max is reflected in visa_types[] for the entry permit category.
CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: General nationalities entry permit capped at 90 days confirmed by live fetch of fsmembassy.fm/fsm-entry-permit-application/.

CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: UK nationals confirmed 30-day visa-free entry by live fetch of gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/federated-states-of-micronesia/entry-requirements. Passport must be valid at least 120 days after arrival date per FCDO. Departure tax USD 20 confirmed.

CONFIRMED 2026-06-29: EU-FSM visa waiver agreement confirmed in effect per EEAS node/10123_en and web search. Ireland and UK excluded per protocols annexed to EU treaties. Agreement signed and entered provisional application 20 September 2016. Remains in effect as of 2026. EUR-Lex page returned empty content on this pass; status confirmed via EEAS search results and RNZ/EEAS press reporting.

CLARIFIED 2026-06-29: The CostaRica_VisaWaiver.pdf on fsmembassy.fm/forms-and-documents-2/ is an OUTBOUND agreement — it covers FSM citizens traveling TO Costa Rica (Costa Rica grants FSM citizens 30 days visa-free), NOT Costa Rican nationals entering FSM. Costa Rican ordinary passport holders fall under FSM's general 30-day visa-free regime and have no special inbound agreement. The prior gap about this document is resolved: it is not an inbound FSM visa policy document.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: No visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA system exists.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Israel conditional access is limited to diplomatic and service passport holders only; ordinary Israeli passport holders fall under the general 30-day visa-free regime.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: No credential-based entry rules found.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: No CBI, RBI, or fast-track programmes exist.
NOTE 2026-06-29: fsmembassy.fm/forms-and-documents-2/ was re-fetched; no new bilateral inbound visa waiver agreements were found. No EFTA-specific bilateral agreements found on official FSM sources.

NOTE (carried from prior run): UK FCDO states passport must be valid for at least 120 days beyond arrival date; travel.state.gov states 180 days for US citizens. These are different authorities for different nationals. No single official FSM government-published passport validity requirement for all nationalities found on fsmembassy.fm.
NOTE (carried from prior run): A USD 20 departure tax applies on exit from each island, payable in cash only; US diplomatic passport holders are exempt per travel.state.gov.
CORRECTED (prior run): EU visa waiver date clarified; source_url updated to authoritative EUR-Lex legal text.
gaps: EFTA Schengen-area countries (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland): No official FSM-EFTA bilateral agreement found on official FSM government sources. Whether EFTA Schengen associates receive enhanced 90-day treatment or fall under the general 30-day visa-free regime remains unconfirmed from official FSM sources. Third-party sources (Wikipedia) suggest 90 days but this is unconfirmed against an official FSM document.
Ireland: Explicitly excluded from the EU-FSM visa waiver. Falls under the general 30-day visa-free regime. No separate bilateral agreement covering ordinary Irish passports was found.
UK: Excluded from EU-FSM visa waiver (Brexit). Falls under the general 30-day visa-free regime, confirmed by UK FCDO.

No official FSM government page enumerates a country-by-country visa exemption list beyond what is stated above. The 'all nationalities 30-day visa-free' rule is stated on the FSM Embassy site without enumerating individual countries.
No information found on any official FSM source regarding RBI, fast-track, skilled worker, startup, or digital nomad visa programmes.
The FSM does not appear to have any dedicated e-visa or ETA portal; no official e-visa system found.
No credential-based conditional entry rules found; entry appears to be based on nationality/citizenship, not possession of third-country visas or permits.

The exact passport validity requirement for general (non-US, non-UK) nationals is not stated on the FSM Embassy website. US travel.state.gov states 180 days for US citizens; UK FCDO states 120 days for UK nationals. The FSM Embassy FAQ/entry page advises only 'valid passport' without specifying a minimum validity period for general nationalities.

The FSM Immigration Regulations PDF (RevFSMImmigrationReg.pdf) is an image-encoded scan and could not be parsed for full regulatory text confirming permit extension limits or prohibited-nationality lists.

EUR-Lex page (CELEX:22016A1025(01)) returned empty content on 2026-06-29 fetch; EU-FSM agreement status confirmed via EEAS search results and secondary sources but the primary legal text could not be re-read on this pass.

outbound_official.note: No official Micronesia government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The FSM Embassy in Washington D.C. (fsmembassydc.org) maintained a page titled 'Visas for FSM Citizens' (http://www.fsmembassydc.org/page/visas-for-fsm-citizens) that is the closest known official origin-side resource, but the domain is currently offline. The FSM Embassy in Honolulu (fsmembassy.fm/forms-and-documents-2/) hosts scanned-image PDFs of individual bilateral visa waiver agreements for FSM citizens travelling to Costa Rica, EU member states, and Mexico, and references a 'List of Countries and Visa Requirements' document without a direct link. The FSM Department of Foreign Affairs (dfa.gov.fm) is inaccessible due to a TLS certificate error. No page on gov.fm or fsmgov.org enumerates outbound visa-free destinations. NOTE: The Costa Rica and Mexico agreements on fsmembassy.fm are OUTBOUND agreements (for FSM citizens traveling abroad), not inbound visa policy for Costa Rican or Mexican nationals entering FSM.
